research_question: What is the human and animal evidence for potential transgenerational inheritance of health effects?

population.name: Population
include: animal models (rodents, zebrafish, Drosophila, C. elegans)
exclude: in vitro cell line studies without whole-organism data

exposure.name: Exposure
include: environmental chemical exposures (pesticides, endocrine disruptors, heavy metals)
nutritional exposures (high-fat diet, caloric restriction, protein restriction)
paternal or maternal exposures before conception or during gestation
exclude: therapeutic drug exposures with no transgenerational follow-up beyond F1
exposures measured only in directly exposed generation (F0) without offspring data

outcome.name: Outcome
include: health effects in unexposed F2+ offspring (true transgenerational; F1 germline transmission counts only if unexposed in utero)
epigenetic changes (DNA methylation, histone modification, miRNA) in offspring
metabolic, reproductive, neurological, or immunological outcomes across generations
exclude: outcomes measured only in directly exposed generation (F0)
